stem: A specialty tea shop sells loose-leaf tea at $12.80 per pound. What is the price of the tea per ounce? (1 pound = 16 ounces)
explanation:
To find the price per ounce, divide the price per pound by the number of ounces in one pound.

$$\frac{\$12.80}{1 \text{ lb}} \times \frac{1 \text{ lb}}{16 \text{ oz}} = \frac{\$12.80}{16 \text{ oz}} = \$0.80 \text{ per oz}$$

The price per ounce is **$0.80**.

distractorRationale.A: Choice A ($0.40) results from using the wrong conversion of 1 pound = 32 ounces instead of 16 ounces, perhaps from confusing pounds with a 2-pound unit: $\$12.80 \div 32 = \$0.40$. The student doubles the correct number of ounces per pound.
distractorRationale.B: Choice B ($0.80) is the correct answer.
distractorRationale.C: Choice C ($1.60) results from using the wrong conversion of 1 pound = 8 ounces instead of 16 ounces: $\$12.80 \div 8 = \$1.60$. The student halves the correct number of ounces per pound, perhaps confusing ounces with another unit.
distractorRationale.D: Choice D ($12.80) results from making no conversion at all — the student stops one step early and reports the given per-pound price as the per-ounce price. This is an early-stop error where the student does not perform the required unit conversion.
